<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# Agent Working Memory Schema

This file defines the YAML schema an AI agent should use as working memory when operating on the **Food Delivery Platform** repository. Each session should instantiate this schema and update it continuously as work progresses.

---

## Schema Definition

```yaml
# ============================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY — Food Delivery Platform
# Instantiate this schema at session start and update in place.
# ============================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. SESSION STATE
# Tracks the current task, its lifecycle phase, and context
# needed to resume after an interruption.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:

  task_id: ""                  # Short identifier for the current task (e.g. "add-driver-rating-endpoint")
  task_description: ""         # Full human-readable description of what is being built or fixed

  status: ""
  # Allowed values:
  #   planning     — decomposing the task, identifying files to touch
  #   in_progress  — actively making changes
  #   blocked      — waiting on a human answer or external dependency
  #   reviewing    — changes made, running tests / awaiting review
  #   complete     — task finished, all tests pass, open_questions resolved

  phase: ""
  # Allowed values:
  #   discovery    — reading code, understanding scope
  #   design       — deciding approach before writing code
  #   implementation
  #   testing
  #   integration  — verifying cross-service behaviour (e.g. RabbitMQ events, X-Customer-Id header)
  #   done

  active_service: ""
  # The primary service being modified in this session. One of:
  #   api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service | frontend
  # Set to "cross-service" when changes span multiple services.

  secondary_services: []
  # Additional services touched in this session (list same allowed values as active_service).

  entry_point_confirmed: false
  # Set true once the agent has verified the relevant entry point for the active_service.
  # Known entry points (from source analysis):
  #   frontend         → frontend/src/main.tsx
  #   api-gateway      → api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/GatewayApplication.kt
  #   order-service    → order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/controller/OrderController.java
  #   inventory-service → inventory-service/main.py
  #   driver-service   → driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go
  #   notification-service → notification-service/src/index.ts

  resumption_notes: ""
  # Free-text notes for resuming an interrupted session. Record the last stable
  # state and what step comes next.


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DECISIONS MADE
# Each entry records a design or implementation decision with
# its rationale, alternatives considered, and any constraints
# that ruled them out.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made:

  - decision_id: ""            # Short slug (e.g. "use-redis-lock-for-stock")
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601 (e.g. "2025-01-15T10:32:00Z")
    service: ""                # Affected service (same allowed values as active_service)
    summary: ""                # One sentence: what was decided
    rationale: ""              # Why this approach was chosen over alternatives
    alternatives_considered:
      - option: ""
        reason_rejected: ""
    constraints_applied:
      # Reference specific forbidden operations or pitfalls that shaped the decision.
      # Examples from this codebase:
      - "Did not bypass Redis distributed lock — inventory-service stock reservation must hold lock"
      - "Did not alter X-Customer-Id header contract — downstream services trust it implicitly"
      - "Did not hardcode secrets — all credentials must come from environment variables"
      - "Did not weaken OrderStatus.isValidTransition — status transitions are strictly validated"
    affected_files: []         # Paths of files that implement this decision
    human_approved: false      # Set true if a human explicitly approved this decision


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FILES MODIFIED
# One entry per file changed in this session. Track enough
# context to understand the change without re-reading the diff.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified:

  - path: ""                   # Repo-relative path (must exist in source_directories)
    service: ""                # Owning service
    language: ""               # typescript | java | kotlin | python | go
    change_type: ""
    # Allowed values:
    #   created | modified | deleted
    change_summary: ""         # What was added, changed, or removed (2–3 sentences max)
    domain_concepts_touched: []
    # List domain concepts affected. Use terms from the verified concept list:
    #   Order | OrderStatus | OrderItem | Restaurant | MenuItem | Driver |
    #   GeoPoint | PaymentIntent | Stock Reservation | Order Event |
    #   Idempotency Key | Customer | Settings | Base
    key_components_touched: []
    # List component names affected. Use names from key_components:
    #   API Gateway | JWT Auth Filter | Order Controller | Order Service |
    #   Order Model | OrderStatus | Payment Gateway | Stripe Payment Gateway |
    #   Inventory Service | Inventory Models | Driver Service |
    #   Notification Service | Frontend App | Auth Context | API Client | Frontend Types
    safety_checks_performed:
      jwt_auth_unchanged: null        # true/false/null — null means not applicable to this file
      stripe_key_handling_unchanged: null
      status_transition_logic_unchanged: null
      redis_lock_preserved: null
      rabbitmq_conventions_unchanged: null
      idempotency_check_preserved: null
      no_secrets_hardcoded: null
      x_customer_id_contract_unchanged: null
    test_file_updated: false   # Was a corresponding test file also modified?
    lombok_annotations_present: null
    # null = not a Java file; true/false = whether Lombok is used in this file
    async_patterns_correct: null
    # null = not Python async code; true/false = whether async/await used correctly (asyncpg)
    driver_store_interface_updated: null
    # null = not Go driver store code; true/false = whether DriverStore interface + mongoDriverStore + mockStore all updated


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. TESTS RUN
# Record every test execution during the session so the agent
# can report final test status and detect regressions.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run:

  - execution_id: ""           # Sequential integer or short slug
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601
    service: ""                # Service under test
    command: ""
    # Use ONLY verified commands from source analysis:
    #   order-service       → cd order-service && mvn -B verify
    #   inventory-service   → pytest --tb=short -q
    #   driver-service      → cd ../driver-service && go test ./...
    #   notification-service → cd ../notification-service && npx jest
    #   full suite          → cd order-service && mvn -B verify && cd ../inventory-service && pytest --tb=short -q && cd ../driver-service && go test ./... && cd ../notification-service && npx jest
    outcome: ""
    # Allowed values: passed | failed | errored | skipped
    failed_tests: []           # List of test names/IDs that failed
    error_output: ""           # Truncated error output if outcome is failed or errored
    new_tests_added: []        # Paths of new test files or test cases added this session
    coverage_delta: ""
    # If coverage tooling is available, note the delta (e.g. "+2.3%").
    # Write "Not determinable from source" if coverage is not configured.
    regression_introduced: false


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. OPEN QUESTIONS
# Items that require human input, cross-service coordination,
# or decisions the agent should not make unilaterally.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
open_questions:

  - question_id: ""            # Short slug (e.g. "confirm-new-status-value")
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601 when the question was raised
    severity: ""
    # Allowed values:
    #   blocking     — work cannot continue until resolved
    #   important    — should be resolved before merging
    #   informational — record for awareness, not blocking
    question: ""               # The specific question needing human review
    context: ""                # Why this question arose (reference code, pitfall, or constraint)
    relevant_pitfalls: []
    # Reference pitfalls from potential_pitfalls that apply. Examples:
    #   - "Order status transitions are strictly validated — adding new statuses requires updating
    #      OrderStatus.isValidTransition and all consuming services"
    #   - "PostgreSQL is shared between order-service and inventory-service — schema changes
    #      can affect both services"
    #   - "The notification service in-memory idempotency Set resets on restart"
    #   - "The frontend dev login generates JWTs with a hardcoded dev secret that must match
    #      the gateway JWT_SECRET in development"
    proposed_answer: ""        # Agent's best guess, if any — clearly marked as unverified
    resolved: false
    resolution: ""             # Fill in when a human answers


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. DOMAIN STATE
# Language- and framework-specific runtime state that the agent
# should track to avoid cross-service inconsistencies.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
domain_state:

  # --- Order lifecycle ---
  order_status:
    known_statuses:
      # Verified from OrderStatus.java source analysis:
      - PENDING
      - ACCEPTED
      - PREPARING
      - READY_FOR_PICKUP
      - IN_TRANSIT
      - DELIVERED
      - CANCELLED
      - REFUNDED
    statuses_added_this_session: []
    # IMPORTANT: If any status is added here, the agent MUST flag an open_question
    # requiring human review of OrderStatus.isValidTransition and all consuming services
    # (notification-service, frontend types, API client).
    transition_logic_file: "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java"

  # --- Payment state ---
  payment:
    stripe_intent_pattern: "pi_xxx"   # PaymentIntent ID format — manual capture flow
    capture_trigger: "DELIVERED status transition"
    refund_trigger: "CANCELLED or REFUNDED status transition"
    gateway_interface: "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/PaymentGateway.java"
    live_implementation: "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/StripePaymentGateway.java"
    stripe_api_key_env_var: "STRIPE_API_KEY"
    webhook_secret_env_var: "STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET"
    # FORBIDDEN: Do not hardcode STRIPE_API_KEY or STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in any source file.

  # --- Messaging / eventing ---
  rabbitmq:
    exchange: "order.events"
    routing_key_pattern: "order.*"
    publisher: "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
    consumer: "notification-service/src/index.ts"
    # FORBIDDEN: Do not rename exchange or routing keys without coordinating all services.
    env_vars:
      - RABBITMQ_HOST
      - RABBITMQ_PORT
      - RABBITMQ_USER
      - RABBITMQ_PASSWORD
      - RABBITMQ_VHOST
      - RABBITMQ_URL
    convention_changes_this_session: false
    # Set true and raise a blocking open_question if any naming convention was changed.

  # --- Stock / inventory ---
  inventory:
    redis_lock_mechanism: "Distributed lock on MenuItem stock_quantity"
    unlimited_stock_sentinel: -1   # stock_quantity == -1 means unlimited
    lock_files:
      - "inventory-service/main
